# ScrollyLite — Reference for AI Agents / LLMs

> Dense, ground-truthed reference for generating correct ScrollyLite code —
> specs, embeddings, custom idioms. Optimized for machine consumption: facts
> and signatures over prose. For human-oriented docs, see `docs/` (linked at
> the end of each section). If something here looks underspecified, the
> linked doc is the authoritative deep-dive — read it before guessing.

## What ScrollyLite is

A declarative scrollytelling grammar. The author writes a sequence of
**steps**, each describing *what the chart looks like at that point* (which
fields are encoded, which rows are filtered/highlighted, how the data is
grouped, how it's oriented). ScrollyLite diffs every consecutive pair of
steps, classifies the difference into one or more **scenes**
(`focus`/`observation`/`granularity`/`guide`), and renders the corresponding
D3 transition automatically. **Never hand-write animation/transition code —
just describe the end state of each step.**

Built on **D3** (rendering, scales, transitions — peer dep, `^7`) and
**Arquero** (data loading/reshaping — peer dep, `^8`). Ships four chart
idioms: `bar`, `line`, `point`, `unit`. Runs as ESM or as a CDN global
(`window.ScrollyLite`).

## Install / load

```sh
npm install scrollylite d3 arquero
```
```js
import * as d3 from "d3";
import * as aq from "arquero";
import { createStory, story, bar, line, point, unit } from "scrollylite";
import "scrollylite/style.css";          // required structural CSS
// import "scrollylite/themes/default.css";  // optional default palette
```
Primary practice: follow D3's ESM-first style. Import dependencies explicitly
and pass both `d3` and `aq` to `createStory`.

Browser CDN ESM (pin versions in production, `@latest` is unstable):
```html
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/scrollylite@0.1.1/dist/scrollylite.css">
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/scrollylite@0.1.1/dist/themes/default.css">
<script type="module">
import * as d3 from "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3@7/+esm";
import * as aq from "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/arquero@8/+esm";
import { createStory, story, bar } from "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/scrollylite@0.1.1/+esm";

const spec = story().data("rows", { values }).step("Baseline", bar("rows").x("category").y("value").key("category")).toSpec();
await createStory(spec, { target: "#app", d3, aq });
</script>
```

Plain script fallback only when module scripts are unavailable:
```html
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/d3@7/dist/d3.min.js"></script>
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/arquero@8/dist/arquero.min.js"></script>
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/scrollylite@0.1.1/dist/scrollylite.global.js"></script>
```

Docs: `docs/getting-started.md`, `docs/for-developers.md`, `docs/for-cdn-users.md`

## End-to-end skeleton (the shape every story has)

```js
const spec = story()                                      // StoryBuilder
  .title("…").description("…")
  .data("rows", { url: "./data.csv", type: "csv" })       // or { values: [...] } / [...]
  .layout("floatToText")                                  // or "textOverVis"
  .view("main", { title: "…", height: 520 })
  .action(["scroll", "tooltip"])                          // default actions for following steps
  .step("Title A", bar("rows").x("category").y("value").key("category"), "Narrative copy")
  .step("Title B", bar("rows").x("category").y("value").key("category").highlight({ category: "B" }))
  .toSpec();                                              // → plain JSON-serializable object

const runtime = await createStory(spec, { target: "#app", d3, aq });
```

`story()` returns a `StoryBuilder`. Every method returns `this` (chainable).
Chart-idiom builders (`bar()`, `line()`, …) are **immutable** — every
chainable method returns a *new* state, so build a `base` chain once and
branch it per step:
```js
const base = bar("rows").x("decade").y("count").sort("year");
story()
  .step("A", base.where({ type: "Hot days" }))
  .step("B", base.where({ type: "Hot days" }).flip())
  .step("C", base.breakdown("type"));
```

## `StoryBuilder` (`story()`) — full method list

| Method | Signature | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `story(initialSpec?)` | seeds `_spec` (deep-cloned) | reuse/extend an existing spec object |
| `.schema(value)` | `string` | sets `$schema` (JSON Schema URI, optional) |
| `.title(value)` | `string` | story title |
| `.description(value)` | `string` | narrative subtitle |
| `.data(name, source)` / `.data({name: source, …})` | see Data below | merges into `spec.data`, additive |
| `.layout(presetOrConfig, options?)` | `"floatToText" \| "textOverVis"` or raw config | merges into `spec.layout`, additive |
| `.view(idOrConfig, config?)` | `(id, {title, height})` or `({id: {…}, …})` | registers named view(s); 2-arg form = single id, map form = many |
| `.action(actions)` | `string \| string[]` | sets default action list for **subsequent** `.step()` calls; recompiles all steps |
| `.step(titleOrDef, view?, options?)` | 3 call shapes — see below | infers transition, appends, recompiles whole list |
| `.steps(definitions[])` | `[{title, view, body}, …]` | replaces entire step list at once |
| `.toSpec()` | → `object` | deep clone of compiled spec, JSON-serializable, idempotent |

`.step()` call shapes:
```js
.step({ title, body, view, authoring })                           // 1: full definition object
.step("Title", chartState, { body: "…", authoring: 'bar(...)' })  // 2: title, state, options
.step("Title", chartState, "Narrative body as plain string")      // 3: title, state, body shorthand
```
`view` = a chart-idiom builder (its `.toSpec()` is called for you) or a raw
view-spec object. The **first step always gets `"enter"` appended** to its
action list automatically — you don't need to set this.

Action values: `"step"` (discrete nav/programmatic playback — default),
`"scroll"` (continuous scroll-scrubbed transition; only meaningful on steps
with an inferred `transition.scene`), `"tooltip"` (hover tooltips), `"enter"`
(auto-played entrance, reserved for step 0, added automatically). Default:
`["step", "tooltip"]`.

## Agent skill seed

If converting this reference into an agent skill, start with this minimal
contract:

```yaml
name: scrollylite
description: Build declarative scrollytelling data stories with ScrollyLite.
use_when:
  - user asks for a scroll-driven narrated visualization
  - user needs a ScrollyLite story spec, example, plugin, or docs explanation
first_read:
  - llms.txt
  - docs/getting-started.md
  - docs/chart-idioms.md
core_rule: Describe chart end states; do not hand-write transition animation.
public_api:
  - story
  - bar
  - line
  - point
  - unit
  - createStory
```

`theme` is **not** a chainable method — pass it in the initial spec object:
```js
story({ theme: { background: "#fafafa", foreground: "#222", accent: "#b05d3b" } })
```
→ sets CSS custom properties `--sl-bg` / `--sl-fg` / `--sl-accent` on `<html>`.

Doc: `docs/story-builder.md`

## Data — `.data(name, source)`

`source` shapes:
```js
{ url: "path/file.csv", type: "csv" }     // or type: "json" — loaded via d3.csv/d3.json
{ values: [{ field: value, … }, …] }      // inline rows, no network
[{ field: value, … }, …]                  // bare array — same as { values }
```
Reference by name from a chart builder: `bar("rowsName")`. Multiple `.data()`
calls merge additively (later names add/override, don't clear earlier ones).

## Transforms (filter, fold, bin, aggregate, sort, …)

Applied via Arquero under the hood; configured through chart-state methods
(`.sort()`, `.where()`/`.filter()`, `.breakdown()`, `.rollup()`, `.segment()`)
or raw `transform` arrays in a hand-authored spec. Exact option shapes for
every transform type (filter, timeUnit, fold, bin, aggregate, sort, limit):
`docs/data-sources-and-transforms.md`

## Chart idioms — shared methods (bar, line, point, unit all extend `ChartState`)

```js
.data(name)                       // switch bound dataset (rare mid-chain)
.x(field, options?) / .y(field, options?)   // string field name (auto-titled) or { field, title, type, scale }
.channel(name, field, options?)   // bind any encoding channel by name (escape hatch)
.color(value | field | config)    // "#hex" literal | "fieldName" | { field, domain, range } | { hue, luminance } composite
.size(field, options?)            // quantitative size channel
.key(fields)                      // semantic identity — "field" or ["field1","field2"]; CRITICAL for clean transitions
.tooltip(items)                   // "field" | ["f1","f2"] | [{field,title,format}, "f2", …]
.sort(field, order?)              // order: "ascending" (default) | "descending"; accumulates
.transition(timing)               // { duration, delay, stagger: { mode, amount, max } }
.filter(selector) / .where(selector)  // {field: value} | {field, equal} | null (clear); BAR OVERRIDES — see below
.highlight(selector, options?)    // keeps all rows, fades non-matching ({opacity}); doesn't remove rows
.guide(config)                    // raw guide escape hatch: { y: { scale: { type: "log" } } }, { flip, staging }
```

`field` for `.x`/`.y`/`.channel` may be a string (auto-titled via `titleize`,
`"hot_days"` → `"Hot days"`) or `{ field, title, type, scale }`. Default
channel `type` differs per idiom (see each idiom's defaults below).

`.key()` — **pick the field that names the "thing" the reader tracks across
steps** (e.g. `decade`, `country`, `product`). A missing/unstable key is the
#1 cause of marks "popping" in/out instead of smoothly morphing. When
`.breakdown()`/`.segment()` adds a dimension, bar auto-extends the key to
`[category, segment]` — you don't need to do it manually.

Doc: `docs/chart-idioms.md` (shared methods + every idiom in full)

## Bar — `bar(dataset)`

Defaults: `mark: "bar"`, `.x()` → `"nominal"`, `.y()` → `"quantitative"`.

```js
const base = bar("rows").x("decade").y("count").sort("year");
```

**`.where()` is special on bar** (overridden, richer than the shared base):
- Accumulates constraints **per field** — `.where({type: "Hot days"})` then
  `.where({type:"Hot days", period:"recent"})` keeps both; a new value for an
  existing field *replaces* just that field's constraint.
- When filtering on a measure-like field (`type`/`kind`/`*_type`/`*_kind`),
  infers `key: [category, measure]` + a `semanticKey` so swapping
  `{type:"Hot days"}` → `{type:"Cold days"}` reads as "same bars, different
  measure" (classified as `focus`, NOT `observation` — see Scenes below) and
  auto-retitles the y-axis to the selected value.
- `.where(null)` clears all constraints.
- Plain non-inferring filter still available via inherited `.filter()`.

```js
.flip(options?)        // vertical↔horizontal; 2-stage staged transition (guide scene)
                       // options: { domain, scale, order: ["y","x"] (default), stage: {duration, stagger} }
.breakdown(segment?, options?)  // one bar → segments (granularity scene)
                       // options: { category, value, by: [cat,seg], layout: "stacked"|"grouped" (default stacked),
                       //           op: "sum"|"mean"|"count"|"min"|"max"|"median" (default sum), title, color, tooltip }
                       // default segment = "type"
.rollup(groupby?, options?)     // segments → fewer/coarser bars, inverse of breakdown (granularity scene)
                       // options/2nd-arg: { groupby/by, value, as, op (default sum), title, color }
.segment(fieldOrConfig?, config?)  // low-level: wide→long folding + custom labels/domains (granularity scene)
                       // config: { fields: [...wideColumns], segment, value, labels: {col: "Label"}, layout, color }
.layout(layout, options?)       // "stacked" | "grouped" — only after breakdown/segment (guide scene)
.stage(order, options?)         // e.g. ["y","x"]; { duration, stagger } — direct staging control (guide scene)
```

Example progression (full): `docs/chart-idioms.md#bar-example-progression`

## Line — `line(dataset)`

Defaults: `mark: "line"`, `.x()` → `"nominal"`, `.y()` → `"quantitative"`.

```js
const base = line("rows").x("decade").y("hot_days").key("decade");

.curve(value)               // "linear" | "monotone" | "natural" | "step" | "basis" | …
.strokeWidth(value) / .pointSize(value)
.flip(options?)             // swap x/y (guide); options: {x, y, order: ["x","y"] default, stage}
.breakdown(field, options?) // 1 line → N series, mode:"series" (granularity); options incl. color
.rollup(groupbyOrOptions?, options?)  // N series → 1 line, mode:"single" (granularity); inverse of breakdown
```

## Point — `point(dataset)`

Defaults: `mark: "point"`, `.x()`/`.y()` → `"quantitative"`.

```js
const base = point("rows").x("tmin").y("tmax").key("decade");

.pointSize(value) / .radius(value)   // radius is alias for pointSize
.flip(options?)             // same shape as line's
.rollup(groupby, options?)  // points → fewer/larger summary circles, mode:"aggregate" (granularity)
                            // options: { key, x: {op}, y: {op}, countAs, sizeRange: [min,max] }
.breakdown(detail?, options?)  // inverse: explode aggregate circles into detail points, mode:"detail" (granularity)
                            // e.g. base.rollup("period").breakdown("year")
```

## Unit — `unit(dataset)`

Pictogram/isotype chart — each row/count drawn as a repeated small mark.
Defaults: `mark: "unit"`. **Only supports `focus` and `guide` scenes** — no
`observation`/`granularity` (its narrative range is "which units are shown"
and "how they're laid out", not aggregation level).

```js
const base = unit("rows").x("year").y("hot_days").key("decade")
  .value("hot_days").label("decade");

.value(field, options?)     // field whose count → number of unit marks; { maxUnits } caps for perf
.label(field)               // text label per group
.columns(value) / .radius(value)   // grid column count, unit circle radius (px)
.group(field, options?)     // grouped-grid layout (guide.layout="groupedGrid"); guide scene
.timeline(field, options?)  // timeline-axis layout (guide.layout="timeline"); guide scene
.dodge(field, options?)     // collision-avoiding layout (guide.layout="dodge"); guide scene
// .group/.timeline/.dodge are MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE — each replaces the guide layout
```

## Scenes — how transitions are inferred (CRITICAL: read before predicting animation)

Every `.step()` diffs the previous chart-state against the current one and
infers one or more scenes from the union of (a) the authoring-operation delta
and (b) a structural spec diff:

| Scene | Triggered by | Question it answers |
|---|---|---|
| `focus` | `.where()`/`.filter()`/`.highlight()`, any filter-transform change | Which rows are emphasized/visible? |
| `observation` | `.x()`/`.y()` bound **field** changes — **except on bar** (folded into `focus`, see Bar `.where()` above) | Which variable is encoded? |
| `granularity` | `.breakdown()`/`.rollup()`/`.segment()` (a real grouping-level change, not just `.layout()`) | What aggregation level is shown? |
| `guide` | `.flip()`, `.guide()`, `.layout()`, `.stage()`, `.group()`/`.timeline()`/`.dodge()` (orientation/scale/staging/layout) | How is the data being read? |

Multiple scenes can co-occur (e.g. `["focus", "guide"]` for a filter+flip in
one step) — ScrollyLite animates the union with the richest plan the idiom
supports. **The first step in a story has no predecessor → `transition:
undefined`** (it's the narrative's start, not a transition).

Per-idiom animation specifics (enter/exit/morph rules, staging order,
key-selection guidance): `docs/scenes-and-transitions.md`

## Layout, scroll, theming

```js
story().layout("floatToText")   // default — two-column: chart floats beside scrolling text
story().layout("textOverVis")   // chart fills sticky stage; text scrolls as overlay on top
story().layout("floatToText", { runtime: { offset: 0.58, nav: true, progress: true,
  scroll: { navigation: { behavior: "smooth", progress: 0.95 } } } })
```
- `offset` (0–1 fraction, px number, or `"NNpx"`/`"NN%"`): viewport line at
  which a step activates; default `0.55`, sweet spot `0.5`–`0.65`.
- `nav`/`progress` (bool): step-dot rail / top progress bar.
- `scroll.navigation`: `{ behavior: "instant"|"smooth", progress: 0–1 }` —
  controls jump-to-step landing point/animation.
- `scroll.{start,end,clamp,snap}` are normalized but **not yet wired to
  runtime behavior** — don't rely on them; use `offset`/`navigation`.
- Action `["scroll", …]` makes a step's transition continuously scrubbed by
  scroll position (vs. `["step", …]` which plays it once on navigation).
  Tune easing via `narrative.action.scroll.ease`: `"linear"` (default),
  `"cubic"`, `"cubicInOut"`, `"cubicOut"`.

Theming = 3 CSS custom properties set from `spec.theme` (`background` →
`--sl-bg`, `foreground` → `--sl-fg`, `accent` → `--sl-accent`). Always load
`scrollylite.css` (structure); `themes/default.css` supplies the default
palette — replace/extend by targeting `--sl-*` and `.sl-*` classes.

Doc: `docs/layouts-themes-and-scrolling.md`

## Runtime — `createStory(spec, options)` → `StoryRuntime`

```ts
async function createStory(spec: object, options: {
  target?: string | Element;   // default "#app"; throws if selector matches nothing
  d3: D3Module;                // required (ESM entry)
  aq?: ArqueroModule;          // required iff spec uses transforms
  debug?: boolean;             // enables debug/inspector affordances
}): Promise<StoryRuntime>
```
Throws synchronously (before async work) on: missing `d3`; missing `aq` when
needed; unresolvable `target`; non-object `spec`. Always `await`/`.catch()`.

```ts
interface StoryRuntime {
  spec: object;                    // fully compiled spec (no authoring shorthand left)
  data: Record<string, any[]>;     // loaded datasets by name
  signature: { index, id?, title, transition: string[], action: string[] }[];
  renderStep(index, { force?, scrollProgress?, direction? }): void;       // jump + render fully
  renderScrollProgress(index, progress: 0-1, direction?: "up"|"down"): void; // scrub a step's transition
  scrollDriver: { type: "native", scrollToStep(index, {behavior?, progress?}), resize(), refresh(), destroy() };
  destroy(): void;                 // tears down listeners; does NOT clear `target`'s DOM
}
```

Doc: `docs/runtime-api.md`

## Registering custom chart idioms

```js
import { defineChartIdiom, registerChartIdiom, registerChartModule, availableChartIdioms } from "scrollylite";

const plugin = defineChartIdiom({
  key: "area",                                          // required, unique
  scenes: ["focus", "guide", "granularity"],            // declare only what you support
  stateOperations: { focus: "filter", guide: "coordinate", granularity: "aggregate" },
  createRenderer: (deps) => areaRenderer,
  createSpecCompiler: (deps) => compileAreaSpec,
  transition: { plan: (src, tgt) => ({...}), intermediateSpecs: (src, tgt, plan) => [...] },
  defaults: { margin: (spec) => ({ top: 24, right: 24, bottom: 36, left: 56 }) }
});
registerChartIdiom(plugin);                  // or registerChartModule({ plugin })
// availableChartIdioms() → ["bar","line","point","unit","area"]
```
Author a chainable builder by extending `IdiomState` (re-exported as
`ChartState` in types): `this.with(patch, sceneTag?)` is the immutable
updater — pass the scene name (`"focus"|"guide"|"granularity"|"observation"`)
as the 2nd arg to make your method participate in scene inference. Built-in
idioms use exactly this API — nothing is special-cased.

Doc: `docs/extending-with-plugins.md`

## Common pitfalls (check these before debugging further)

1. **Marks pop instead of morph** → missing/unstable `.key()`. Pick a field
   that names the tracked entity consistently across steps.
2. **No `observation` scene fires on a bar measure-swap** → expected; bar
   folds measure changes into `focus` (see Bar `.where()`).
3. **`.layout()` alone doesn't trigger `granularity`** → correct; pure layout
   switches (stacked↔grouped) are `guide`, not a grouping-level change.
4. **`scroll.start/end/clamp/snap` have no effect** → not yet implemented;
   use `offset` + `navigation` instead.
5. **`"ScrollyLite requires D3…"` / `"…require Arquero…"`** → pass `d3`
   (always) / `aq` (whenever transforms are used) explicitly to `createStory`
   on the ESM entry — only the global/CDN build auto-falls-back to globals.
6. **`"ScrollyLite target not found: …"`** → the target element must exist in
   the DOM *before* `createStory` runs.
7. **Theming via `.theme()`** → doesn't exist; pass `theme` in the initial
   `story({ theme: {...} })` spec object.
8. **`unit` idiom ignores `.breakdown()`/`.rollup()`/`.x()`-field changes for
   scene purposes** → by design; it only models `focus`/`guide`.

`docs/layouts-themes-and-scrolling.md` · `docs/scenes-and-transitions.md` ·
`docs/runtime-api.md` · `docs/extending-with-plugins.md` ·
`docs/for-developers.md` · `docs/for-cdn-users.md` · `src/charts/README.md`
